openmeter/ingest/kafkaingest/serializer.purpose: Defines the Serializer interface and its JSON implementation for converting CloudEvents into Kafka key/value byte pairs. The key encodes a dedupe.Item (namespace+source+id) for sink-worker Redis deduplication; the value encodes a flat CloudEventsKafkaPayload with unix-timestamp time.

patterns.name: Serializer interface contract
patterns.description: Every serializer must implement SerializeKey(topic, namespace, event), SerializeValue(topic, event), GetFormat(), GetKeySchemaId(), GetValueSchemaId(). JSON serializer returns -1 for schema IDs (no registry).

patterns.name: Key encodes dedupe.Item
patterns.description: SerializeKey must produce []byte(dedupe.Item{Namespace, ID, Source}.Key()). The sink worker's Redis deduplication depends on this exact format — changing it breaks deduplication.
patterns.example: dedupeItem := dedupe.Item{Namespace: namespace, ID: ev.ID(), Source: ev.Source()}; return []byte(dedupeItem.Key()), nil

patterns.name: CloudEventsKafkaPayload wire format
patterns.description: SerializeValue must go through toCloudEventsKafkaPayload then json.Marshal. Time is unix int64 (seconds, timezone lost). Data is a JSON string. FromKafkaPayloadToCloudEvents is the inverse — used by the sink worker.

patterns.name: Optional data field handling
patterns.description: When ev.Data() is empty, Data field is left as empty string. When present, parse via ev.DataAs(&data) then re-marshal to JSON string. Parse failures must return an error — never silently drop.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Use var _ Serializer = ConcreteType{} at package level to assert implementation at compile time.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Serializer interface, CloudEventsKafkaPayload wire struct, toCloudEventsKafkaPayload, FromKafkaPayloadToCloudEvents, and ValidateKafkaPayloadToCloudEvent. Shared by producer and sink consumer paths.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromKafkaPayloadToCloudEvents is consumed by the sink worker — changing field names or unix-timestamp semantics breaks deserialization on the consumer side.

key_file_guides.role: Concrete JSONSerializer: no schema registry, GetKeySchemaId/GetValueSchemaId return -1, key derived from dedupe.Item.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Key format must match dedupe.Item.Key() output exactly ('namespace-source-id') — the sink worker Redis deduplication depends on it.

key_file_guides.role: Table-driven round-trip tests for toCloudEventsKafkaPayload, FromKafkaPayloadToCloudEvents, and SerializeKey format assertion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestSerializeKey asserts key = 'namespace-source-id'; any dedupe.Item.Key() format change must be reflected here.

anti_patterns: Adding schema-registry-aware serializers without implementing the full Serializer interface (GetKeySchemaId/GetValueSchemaId must return real schema IDs)
Storing timezone-aware time in CloudEventsKafkaPayload.Time — field is unix int64, timezone is intentionally dropped
Embedding namespace in the value payload — namespace belongs only in the key via dedupe.Item
Silently dropping data parse errors in toCloudEventsKafkaPayload — always return the error
Bypassing toCloudEventsKafkaPayload in SerializeValue — the sink worker's FromKafkaPayloadToCloudEvents expects exactly this struct

decisions.decision: Key derived from dedupe.Item (namespace+source+id) rather than event ID alone
decisions.rationale: Guarantees global uniqueness across namespaces for Redis-backed deduplication in the sink worker

decisions.decision: Data stored as JSON string inside CloudEventsKafkaPayload rather than raw bytes
decisions.rationale: CloudEvents data is JSON-only in this system; string encoding avoids base64 overhead and keeps the payload human-readable in Kafka

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new Serializer (e.g. Avro) following the existing pattern

openmeter/ingest/kafkaingest/topicresolv.purpose: Provides the Resolver interface and its NamespacedTopicResolver implementation, which maps a namespace string to a Kafka topic name via a printf-style template (e.g. 'om_%s_events'). Used by the Kafka ingest collector to determine the per-namespace target topic.

patterns.name: Resolver interface for topic lookup
patterns.description: All topic resolution must go through the Resolver interface: Resolve(ctx context.Context, namespace string) (string, error). Callers must never hard-code topic names.
patterns.example: type Resolver interface { Resolve(ctx context.Context, namespace string) (string, error) }

patterns.name: Template-based namespace-to-topic mapping
patterns.description: NamespacedTopicResolver uses fmt.Sprintf(template, namespace). Template must contain exactly one %s placeholder. Constructor validates non-empty template but not placeholder count.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: var _ Resolver = (*NamespacedTopicResolver)(nil) ensures the concrete type satisfies the interface at compile time.

patterns.name: Context threading
patterns.description: Resolve accepts context.Context even though NamespacedTopicResolver ignores it. Always pass the caller's ctx to support future async/DB-backed implementations.
patterns.example: func (r NamespacedTopicResolver) Resolve(_ context.Context, namespace string) (string, error)

key_file_guides.role: Pure interface definition only. New resolver implementations belong in separate files in this package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic to resolver.go — keep it as a pure interface definition.

key_file_guides.role: Single-template resolver; constructor rejects empty templates. The only current Resolver implementation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Template must have exactly one %s; constructor does not validate placeholder count — a template with zero or two %s arguments causes a runtime fmt.Sprintf format error.

anti_patterns: Hard-coding topic names in the collector instead of using Resolver.Resolve
Passing a template string with zero or multiple %s placeholders to NewNamespacedTopicResolver
Implementing Resolver without accepting context.Context — the interface requires it for future async/DB-backed implementations
Calling Resolve with context.Background() in application code — always propagate the caller's context

decisions.decision: Topic name derived from a template rather than a static config string
decisions.rationale: Enables per-namespace Kafka topic isolation (multi-tenant ingest) without requiring config changes per new namespace

decisions.decision: Resolver is an interface, not a function type
decisions.rationale: Allows future implementations (e.g. DB-backed dynamic routing) to be swapped in via DI without changing the collector
